Records.description: Four-loop banana integral family with two distinct internal masses in the 4-1 configuration: four propagators carry mass $m_1$ and one carries mass $m_2$. Fourteen denominators (five propagators + nine ISPs). Computed in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. The underlying geometry is a Calabi-Yau three-fold with Hodge numbers $h^{(3,0)}=h^{(0,3)}=1$, $h^{(2,1)}=h^{(1,2)}=2$. Nine master integrals: two tadpoles, six in the derivative block on the maximal cut (involving Yukawa three-couplings $C_{i,j,k}$), and one decoupling integral. The canonical differential equation is derived locally at the MUM-point $(z_1,z_2)=(0,0)$. The Kira 2.0 IBP reduction package was used. All auxiliary functions $F_{ij}$ have integer coefficients when expanded at the MUM-point. This is the first $\varepsilon$-factorised form for this integral.

Four-loop equal-momentum banana with four $m_1$ propagators and one $m_2$ propagator; 9 masters. At $\varepsilon=0$ the six non-tadpole masters compute periods of a two-parameter family of Calabi-Yau threefolds. Canonical intersection matrix $\Delta^{(4-1)}=K_7$ on the maximal cut; rotation $U_t=O R$ from Proposition 1.
Records.familyName: four-loop banana with two unequal masses
